title: Unknown File Data, Versioned Schemas, Atomic Save, Migration, and Restore · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for unknown file data, versioned schemas, atomic save, migration, and restore and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, compile diagnostics, explicit non-claims, and named browser, TypeScript, Node, network, filesystem, terminal, load, accessibility, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for versioned validated collection persistence adapter. Learners progress through predi…
objectives: Keep browser work on pure string or byte models and isolate Node filesystem effects behind injected asynchronous ports.
Define an explicit versioned JSON schema for metadata, records, provenance, and integrity with deterministic serialization.
Bound bytes, parse into unknown, validate own keys and every member, reject duplicates and future versions, then commit state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
